title: elymus stewartii
intro: Elymus stewartii, also known as Stewart's wheatgrass or wild rye, is a type of grass native to the western regions of North America. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been shown to be beneficial in treating various health conditions. The plant has been used to support digestive health, reduce inflammation, and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu, among other health benefits. Herbal preparations of Elymus stewartii can be made into teas, tinctures, or infusions, and are often used to promote overall well-being and support the immune system.
